have h₁ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
    -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic to deduce that its restriction to the imaginary axis is differentiable
    have h₂ : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) F := F_holo
    -- The restriction to the imaginary axis is differentiable as a function of a real variable
    have h₃ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
      -- Use the holomorphicity of F to deduce differentiability
      apply DifferentiableAt.re
      apply DifferentiableAt.comp (differentiableAt_id' : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => t) t)
      -- Use the fact that the restriction to the imaginary axis is holomorphic
      have h₄ : DifferentiableAt ℂ (fun z : ℂ => F z) (Complex.I * t) := by
        apply h₂.differentiableAt
      -- Convert the complex differentiability to real differentiability
      convert h₄.comp (differentiableAt_id'.smul differentiableAt_const _) using 1
      <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, mul_comm]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> norm_num
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, mul_comm]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> norm_num
      <;> linarith
    exact h₃
  -- Since FReal is defined as the real part of the restriction of F to the imaginary axis, it is differentiable
  have h₂ : FReal = (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) := by
    funext t
    rfl
  rw [h₂]
  exact h₁

3. The real part `(F(t * I)).re` is then differentiable as `ℝ → ℝ`.

    have : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) F := F_holo

    apply DifferentiableAt.re

    have h3 : DifferentiableAt ℂ (fun z : ℂ => F z) (Complex.mk 0 t) := h1
    have h4 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.mk 0 t))) t := by
      -- Use the fact that the function is differentiable in the complex plane to deduce differentiability in the real part
      have h5 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (Complex.mk 0 t : ℂ)) t := by

      have h6 : DifferentiableAt ℂ (fun z : ℂ => F z) (Complex.mk 0 t) := h1
      exact h6.comp t h5
    exact h4
  -- Simplify the expression to match the definition of FReal
  have h3 : (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.mk 0 t)).re) = FReal := by
    funext t
    simp [FReal, Complex.ext_iff, Complex.ofReal_zero]
    <;> rfl
  rw [h3] at h2
  exact h2

1. Prove that `(fun t : ℝ ↦ (t : ℂ) * Complex.I)` is differentiable at `t : ℝ`.
2. Use that `F : ℂ → ℂ` is differentiable everywhere (by `F_holo`, since complex-differentiable implies differentiable).
3. Deduce that `(fun t : ℝ ↦ F (t * Complex.I))` is differentiable at `t : ℝ` (by composition of differentiable functions).
4. Deduce that `(fun t : ℝ ↦ (F (t * Complex.I)).re)` is differentiable at `t : ℝ`, because the real part of a differentiable function is differentiable. 

But perhaps Lean requires more steps. 

Looking at the Mathlib, `Complex.differentiableAt_iff` might help, and perhaps `HasDerivAt`.

have h₁ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun x : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis x).re) t := by
    -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic to show that its restriction to the imaginary axis is differentiable
    have h₂ : MDifferentiable 𝓝[>] t (𝓝 (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)))) (fun x : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (x : ℂ)))) := by
      -- Prove that the restriction is holomorphic (hence differentiable)
      have h₃ : MDifferentiable 𝓝[>] t 𝓝 (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))) (fun x : ℝ => F (Complex.I * (x : ℂ))) := by
        -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic to deduce differentiability of the restriction
        have h₄ : MDifferentiable 𝓝[>] t 𝓝 (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))) (fun x : ℝ => F (Complex.I * (x : ℂ))) := by
          -- Apply the chain rule for holomorphic functions
          apply (F_holo.differentiableAt (isOpen_Ioi.mem_nhds ht)).comp t (differentiableAt_id.const_mul (1 : ℝ)).differentiableWithinAt
          <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
          <;> norm_num
          <;> simp_all [isOpen_Ioi.mem_nhds]
          <;> norm_num
          <;> linarith
        exact h₄
      exact h₃
    -- Convert the complex differentiability to real differentiability of the real part
    have h₃ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun x : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (x : ℂ))).re) t := by
      -- Use the fact that the real part of a holomorphic function is differentiable
      have h₄ : (fun x : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (x : ℂ))).re) = (fun x : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (x : ℂ)) : ℂ).re) := by simp [Complex.ext_iff]
      rw [h₄]
      apply DifferentiableAt.comp t (differentiableAt_id.const_mul (1 : ℝ)).differentiableAt
      apply DifferentiableAt.re
      -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic
      have h₅ : DifferentiableAt 𝕜 (fun x : ℂ => F x) (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) := by
        apply F_holo.differentiableAt
        <;> simp [isOpen_Ioi.mem_nhds ht]
        <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try simp_all [isOpen_Ioi.mem_nhds]) <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try linarith)
      -- Compose with the linear map z ↦ i z
      have h₆ : DifferentiableAt 𝕜 (fun x : ℝ => (Complex.I * (x : ℂ))) (t : ℝ) := by
        apply DifferentiableAt.const_mul (differentiableAt_id)
      -- Use the chain rule for complex functions
      have h₇ : DifferentiableAt 𝕜 (fun x : ℝ => F (Complex.I * (x : ℂ))) (t : ℝ) := by
        apply DifferentiableAt.comp (t : ℝ) h₅ h₆
      -- Convert complex differentiability to real differentiability
      exact differentiableAt_id.toComplex_real.comp (t : ℝ) |>.trans h₇
    -- Relate the real part of the restriction to FReal
    have h₄ : (fun x : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (x : ℂ))).re) = (fun x : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis x).re) := by
      funext x
      simp [resToImagAxis]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
      <;> norm_num
    rw [h₄] at h₃
    exact h₃
  -- Conclude the proof by showing that FReal is the same as the function we've shown to be differentiable
  have h₂ : (fun x : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis x).re) = FReal := by
    funext x
    rfl
  rw [h₂] at h₁
  exact h₁

have h₁ : DifferentiableAt ℝ FReal t := by
    have h₂ : FReal = fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re := rfl
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic to deduce that its restriction to the imaginary axis is differentiable
    have h₃ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
      -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic to deduce that its restriction to the imaginary axis is differentiable
      have h₄ : MDifferentiable 𝓝[≠] 𝓝 (F : ℂ → ℂ) := by
        -- F is holomorphic, hence it is differentiable everywhere
        exact F_holo.differentiable le_rfl
      -- Use the fact that the real part of a holomorphic function is differentiable
      have h₅ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
        -- Use the fact that the restriction of a holomorphic function to the imaginary axis is differentiable
        have h₆ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) t := by
          -- Use the fact that the real part of a holomorphic function is differentiable
          have h₇ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) : ℂ)) t := by
            -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic to deduce that it is differentiable at Complex.I * t
            have h₈ : DifferentiableAt ℂ (F : ℂ → ℂ) (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) := by
              -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic everywhere
              exact DifferentiableAt.of_isOpen F_holo.differentiable (isOpen_univ.mem_nhds (by trivial))
            -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable
            have h₉ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (Complex.I * (t : ℂ) : ℂ)) t := by
              -- The function t ↦ I * t is differentiable
              have h₁₀ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (Complex.I * (t : ℂ) : ℂ)) t := by
                simpa using (differentiableAt_id : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (t : ℂ)) t).const_mul Complex.I
              exact h₁₀
            -- Use the chain rule to deduce that F ∘ (t ↦ I * t) is differentiable
            have h₁₁ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) : ℂ)) t := by
              -- Apply the chain rule
              apply DifferentiableAt.comp _ h₈ h₉
            exact h₁₁
          -- Use the fact that the real part of a differentiable complex function is differentiable
          have h₁₂ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) : ℂ)) t := h₇
          have h₁₃ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) t := by
            -- The real part of a differentiable complex function is differentiable
            apply DifferentiableAt.re
            exact h₁₂
          exact h₁₃
        -- The restriction of F to the imaginary axis is the same as F ∘ (t ↦ I * t)
        have h₁₄ : (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) = (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) := by
          funext t
          simp [ResToImagAxis]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
          <;> norm_num
          <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
          <;> norm_num
          <;> linarith
        rw [h₁₄]
        exact h₆
      exact h₅
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
    -- Since F is holomorphic and resToImagAxis is smooth, its real part is differentiable.
    apply DifferentiableAt.comp _ (differentiableAt_id : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => t) t)
    -- The resToImagAxis operation is smooth, and F is holomorphic, so the composition is differentiable.
    -- Here, we assume that the real part of F is differentiable when restricted to the imaginary axis.
    have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun z : ℂ => F z) := by
      -- F is holomorphic, so it is differentiable in the complex sense.
      have h₂ : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) F := F_holo
      exact h₂.differentiable
    -- The composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
    -- The real part of a holomorphic function is harmonic and thus differentiable.
    -- The restriction to the imaginary axis is also differentiable because it is a smooth curve.
    -- Therefore, the composition of these operations is differentiable.
    have h₂ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => F (↑t * Complex.I)) t := by
      apply DifferentiableAt.comp _ (differentiableAt_id : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => ↑t * Complex.I) t)
      -- The complex function F is holomorphic, and the curve t ↦ t * I is smooth, so the composition is differentiable.
      have h₃ : DifferentiableAt ℂ (fun z : ℂ => F z) (↑t * Complex.I) := by
        apply h₁.differentiableAt
      simpa using h₃
    -- The real part of a differentiable complex function is differentiable.
    convert h₂.re using 1 <;> simp [FReal]
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two, pow_three]) <;>
    (try ring_nf) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two, pow_three]) <;>
    (try linarith)
  -- Since the real part of F is differentiable, FReal is differentiable.
  convert h using 1
  <;> simp [FReal]
  <;>
  (try simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two, pow_three]) <;>
  (try ring_nf) <;>
  (try norm_num) <;>
  (try simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two, pow_three]) <;>
  (try linarith)
